data.title: Essity AB (publ) GAAP EPS of SEK4.69, revenue of SEK34.69M
data.description:
• Essity AB (publ) press release (ETTYF): Q4 GAAP EPS of SEK4.69.
  • Revenue of SEK34.69M.
  • Organic sales growth amounted to -1.1%, of which volume accounted for -0.2% and price/mix -0.9%
  • EBITA increased 9% to SEK 5,005m (4,585)
  • EBITA excl. IAC increased 3% to SEK 5,117m (4,969). Excl. currency translation effects, EBITA excl. IAC increased 12%, corresponding to SEK 594m.
  • EBITA margin excl. IAC increased 1.6 percentage points to 14.7% (13.1)
  • ROCE increased to 17.6% (15.8) and ROCE excl. IAC increased to 18.0% (17.1)
  • Profit for the period, total operations, increased to SEK 3,224m (2,893)

data.title: Health care sector among 2025’s weakest, returning just over 15%
data.description:
The healthcare sector (XLV), (VHT), (IYH), (IXJ) ranked among the weakest sectors in 2025, posting a gross total return of just 15.08% and placing 14th out of 20 industries analyzed, according to a report from BestBrokers.

The research team examined global performance using the MSCI ACWI Indexes, which cover 20 industries across 49 countries, to provide a data-driven view of sectoral performance and the firms shaping market trends.

The underperformance surprised many observers, as health care was outpaced not only by expected leaders such as semiconductors (SMH), (SOXX), (XSD) and materials (XLB), (MXI), (IYM), (VAW) but also by sectors often considered less directly tied to innovation, including financials (XLF), (IYF), industrials (XLI), (IYJ), utilities (IDU), (XLU), and select energy producers (POWR), (RNRG), according to the BestBrokers analysis.

The contrast was particularly stark when compared to commodity-driven rallies, with silver (SIL), (SILJ) and gold mining (GDX) generating extraordinary gains of 207.42% and 175.69%, respectively—roughly ten times higher than healthcare’s return.

The weak returns occurred despite significant therapeutic innovation in high-demand areas, creating an apparent paradox for the sector. Danish pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk (NVO) is rolling out its FDA-approved Wegovy oral GLP-1 pill, while Eli Lilly (LLY) – which led the sector with a market cap approaching $1T at $965.4B – is seeing strong forecasts for next-generation obesity treatments.

In addition, the health care sector (XLV) ranks fifth in average market capitalization, with companies averaging $38.56B across 199 constituents, though over a 10-year horizon the sector’s annualized return moderates to just 8.40%, reflecting slower, steady growth compared with commodities and IT sectors.

“Entering 2026, healthcare (XLV) is navigating a convergence of structural growth and strategic repositioning,” said Alan Goldberg, analyst and author at BestBrokers.

According to Goldberg, returns will likely become more differentiated at the company level, favoring firms with robust R&D capacity and established commercial execution that are capable of managing regulatory complexity and pricing pressures rather than riding broad sector momentum.

data.title: Elanco upgraded, Zoetis downgraded at Piper Sandler
data.description:
• Piper Sandler upgraded Elanco Animal Health (ELAN) to overweight from neutral citing inroads it has made in corporate accounts.
  • The firm also downgraded Zoetis (ZTS) to neutral from overweight saying that the present consumer spending environment is not conducive for the company.
  • The firm upped its Elanco price target to $30 from $24 (~23% upside based on Jan. 21 close). For Zoetis, the target was cut to $135 to $190 (~8% upside).
  • Analyst David Westenberg noted that "some of the [corporate] accounts told us Elanco will move all the way up to number two on who they want to work with," adding that there was little pushback on the idea the company will raise prices on Befrena (tirnovetmab), its treatment for itching associated with canine allergic and atopic dermatitis.
  • For Zoetis, it has "by far the best portfolio of products in animal health Rx, and we believe this will be true for another ten years," Westernberg added. "Saying that, the company is in an innovation air pocket that could last one to two years."

data.title: Terrestrial Energy surges on DoE agreement for TEFLA project
data.description:
Terrestrial Energy (IMSR) +22.3% in Thursday's trading after saying it executed an Other Transaction Authority agreement with the U.S. Department of Energy for Project TEFLA, a pilot production facility that will demonstrate Integral Molten Salt Reactor Fuel Salt technology.

The developer of small modular nuclear power plants said the agreement marks an important milestone in its engagement with the Fuel Line Pilot Program, which seeks to address a U.S. shortage of domestic nuclear fuel resources by developing or building fuel production lines to increase production capacity.

Terrestrial Energy (IMSR) said Project TEFLA will support its commercial IMSR plant development and future deployments by demonstrating the company's proprietary IMSR Fuel Salt production technology and produce IMSR fuel at pilot plant scale, synthesizing the fuel to precise reactor requirements using readily available standard-assay low-enriched uranium enriched to less than 5% U235 as feedstock.

The company said its commercial IMSR plant is designed to produce 822 MWth of flexible, low-cost clean firm energy for industrial heat applications and electricity generation.

data.title: ACNB GAAP EPS of $1.04, revenue of $37.18M
data.description:
• ACNB press release (ACNB): Q4 GAAP EPS of $1.04.
  • Revenue of $37.18M.
  • Total non-performing loans to total loans, net of unearned income, was 0.46% at December 31, 2025 compared to 0.40% at December 31, 2024
  • Net charge-offs to average loans outstanding were 0.01% for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025 compared to 0.02% for the twelve months ended December 31, 2024
  • Tangible common equity to tangible assets ratio1 of 10.60% at December 31, 2025 compared to 10.72% at December 31, 2024. The net unrealized loss on the available for sale securities portfolio was $24.2 million at December 31, 2025 compared to a net unrealized loss of $47.7 million at December 31, 2024
  • ACNB repurchased 264,393 shares of ACNB common stock in open market transactions for the twelve months ended December 31, 2025

data.title: Celestica shares sink on report Google's TPU servers may be assembled elsewhere
data.description:
• Celestica (CLS) shares sank nearly 8% on Thursday after Digitimes reported that Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) tensor processing unit servers may be assembled by another supplier.
  • Google has expanded assembly orders for its L10-L11 TPU servers to Inventec and other Taiwanese manufacturers, the news outlet added, citing sources. The servers beyond the L10 were previously assembled by Celestica.
  • Canada-based Celestica did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Cedar Creek Partners adds EXCE; exits PROM
data.description:
• Cedar Creek Partners Fund posted a 2.4% return in Q4 2025, slightly underperforming the S&P 500 at 2.7%, while outperforming the Russell 2000, which gained 2.2% over the same period.
  • During Q4 2025, the fund added a new position in Exco Resources (expert: EXCE). 
  • In Q4 2025, the fund exited Propel Media (expert: PROM).
  • Source.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile schedules late February for BlueBird 7 launch
data.description:
Shares of AST SpaceMobile Inc. (ASTS) are up 4.5% in early open market trading on Thursday after the company announced that it has scheduled the BlueBird 7 mission launch for late February.

The satellites will be launched from Launch Complex 36 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station on Blue Origin’s (BORGN) New Glenn launch vehicle. Future missions on New Glenn are expected to deliver up to 8 next-generation BlueBirds per flight.

BlueBird 7, which is similar to BlueBird 6, is the second satellite in AST SpaceMobile’s next-generation campaign. It measures nearly 2,400 square feet and features the "largest commercial communications array in low Earth orbit," 3.5 times larger than BlueBirds 1-5, the company said.

data.title: Northern Trust Q4 earnings, revenue beats helped by NII, servicing fee growth
data.description:
Northern Trust (NTRS) climbed 6.3% in Thursday morning trading after delivering a double beat in Q4, helped by better-than-expected net interest income growth and strong asset servicing income.

Q4 GAAP EPS of $2.42, vs. $2.35 consensus, rose from $2.29 in Q3 and $2.26 in Q4 2024. The current quarter included a pretax expense of $19.2M related to Visa (V) class B swaps, a $58.8M pretax expense for severance-related charges, and a $9.5M pretax release of FDIC special assessment reserve.

Total revenue (fully taxable equivalent) of $2.14B, beating the average analyst estimate of $2.06B, increased from $2.03B in the previous quarter and $1.97B in the year-ago period.

"Mid-single-digit trust fee growth, double-digit net interest income, and disciplined expense management drove positive operating leverage of four points in the fourth quarter and more than two points for the full year, both excluding notables," said Chairman and CEO Michael O'Grady.

Net interest income ((FTE)) of $654.3M, exceeding the Visible Alpha consensus of $601.2M, grew from $596.3M in Q3 and $574.3M in the year-ago Q4. Net interest margin ((FTE)) improved to 1.81% from 1.70% in Q3 and 1.71% in Q4 2024.

Client assets under custody/administration hit $18.7T, up 3% Q/Q and 11% Y/Y.

Trust, investment, and other servicing fees totaled $1.31B, up 3% Q/Q and 7% Y/Y.

Asset servicing income before taxes ((FTE)) of $325.8M rose 11% Q/Q and 23% Y/Y, while wealth management income before taxes ((FTE)) was essentially flat Q/Q and slipped 3% Y/Y.

Total average deposits of $119.8B gained 3% Q/Q and 6% Y/Y. Average total loans of $40.6B fell 2% Q/Q and 1% Y/Y.

Q4 noninterest expense of $1.50B grew 5% Q/Q and 9% Y/Y, with compensation expense of $686.8M increasing 10% from the prior quarter and 15% from a year ago.
